params.text:
High-quality chest compressions

Chest compressions keep blood moving to the brain and heart. Quality matters — get these five things right:

  • Position: kneel beside the person and place the heel of one hand in the centre of the chest (on the lower half of the breastbone). Place your other hand on top and interlock your fingers.
  • Posture: keep your arms straight and your shoulders directly above your hands, using your body weight to press down.
  • Depth: press down 5–6 cm — at least 5 cm but not more than 6 cm.
  • Rate: compress at a rate of 100–120 per minute.
  • Recoil: allow the chest to come back up fully after each compression — let it recoil completely and do not lean on the chest between compressions.

Keep interruptions to a minimum. If someone else is trained, swap over roughly every two minutes to avoid tiring, as tired rescuers push less effectively.

params.taskDescription: Drag the correct figures and words into the gaps to describe high-quality chest compressions.
params.textField: Place your hands in the *centre* of the chest. Compress to a depth of *5-6* centimetres, at a rate of *100-120* per minute. Allow the chest to fully *recoil* after each push. Keep *interruptions* to a minimum and swap rescuer every *two* minutes.
